meaning: adj.欺诈的,欺骗性的,骗得的
examples: As is announced in the papers, our country has launched a largescale movement against smuggling and fraudulent activities in foreign currency exchange deals.
There is no evidence that the broker was in league with the fraudulent vendor.
Review the file for any fraudulent preferences or reviewable transactions.
Even worse, many of the mortgages were based on information the loan originator knew was fraudulent.
In a statement on Monday, the Dutch Justice Ministry published a list of the fraudulent certificates.
